About Me

Built for Intelligence.
Designed for Impact.

A Computer Science and Engineering graduate (Class of 2026) specialized in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning at GITAM Deemed to be University, Bengaluru — with a CGPA of 8.94. The academic foundation is strong; the ambition, even stronger.

With hands-on experience in building intelligent applications across Natural Language Processing, Computer Vision, and real-time gesture recognition, the work is defined by a singular standard: clean, scalable, and purposeful solutions. Every system engineered to perform at the highest level.

From interning with Intel through the Intel Unnati Industrial Training Program to participating in national-level hackathons and publishing research, the journey reflects a relentless pursuit of mastery — not merely competency.

Work Experience

The Record

A lineage of purposeful work—each engagement reflecting precision, curiosity, and a relentless pursuit of what is possible.

May 2025 — July 2025

Intel Unnati Industrial Training 2025

AI / ML Intern  •  Bengaluru, Karnataka

  • Developed and integrated AI/ML models for an AI-Powered Interactive Learning Assistant, enhancing personalized classroom learning experiences.
  • Implemented Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to understand, process, and generate context-aware responses to user queries in real time.
  • Built Computer Vision modules using OpenCV for real-time face detection, attendance tracking, and student engagement analysis.
  • Designed and deployed scalable backend services using Flask and FastAPI, integrating AI models through RESTful APIs for production-ready applications.
  • Conducted Large Language Model (LLM) experimentation and evaluation to improve response accuracy, contextual understanding, and overall system performance.
  • Optimized model inference pipelines and system workflows to ensure low-latency responses and seamless user interaction.
2025

Aptean AI Hackathon 2025

National Participant  •  Rajaji Nagar, Bengaluru

  • Selected as a National Participant in Aptean AI Hackathon 2025 to develop scalable AI solutions for real-world industrial challenges.
  • Designed and prototyped AI-driven applications, demonstrating strong expertise in AI/ML development and problem-solving.
  • Integrated backend services and intelligent workflows to build functional, end-to-end prototype solutions under strict deadlines.
  • Collaborated in a fast-paced competitive environment, showcasing rapid prototyping, teamwork, and technical adaptability.
2025

Smart India Hackathon 2025

Participant  •  GITAM University, Bengaluru

  • Participated in Smart India Hackathon 2025, addressing a national-level problem statement focused on underground mine safety and environmental monitoring.
  • Developed an IoT-based Smart Environmental Monitoring System using Arduino and multiple sensors to track gas concentration, temperature, and humidity in real time.
  • Engineered sensor data acquisition and processing pipelines, enabling continuous monitoring and reliable transmission of environmental parameters.
  • Built interactive dashboards for live visualization, analysis, and alerting to support proactive safety management in mining environments.
  • Delivered a fully functional prototype within a 24 hour hackathon sprint, demonstrating rapid development, teamwork, and problem-solving capabilities.

Projects

Engineered Works

Each project a distinct expression of technical discipline — conceived with purpose, built to endure, refined to excellence.

01 IoT · Arduino · Sensors · Python · Real-Time Dashboard

IoT Smart Environmental Monitoring System
for Underground Coal Mines

Smart India Hackathon 2025 — GITAM University, Bengaluru. An IoT-based environmental monitoring system engineered for underground coal mines, tracking gas concentrations (CO, methane), temperature, and humidity in real time via Arduino sensor pipelines. Threshold-based alert system triggers automated warnings when parameters exceed safety limits. Integrated live dashboards for remote monitoring with sub-second latency. Functional hardware and software prototype delivered under a 36-hour hackathon sprint, demonstrating end-to-end capability from sensor data acquisition to cloud-based visualisation.

Explore Project
02 Python · Flask · OpenCV · NLP · REST APIs · Computer Vision

AI-Powered Interactive
Learning Assistant for Classrooms

Developed a multimodal AI classroom assistant integrating Speech-to-Text, OCR Text Extraction, NLP, Computer Vision, and Flask to answer student queries in real time from voice, text, and images. Optimized inference with Intel OpenVINO for faster CPU performance and efficient deployment on resource-constrained systems.
